summary: Legacy validated record without machine-checkable validation proof. Sets the official DisabledComponents value to 0xFF, which tells Windows to disable all IPv6 components except the loopback interface. Microsoft warns that disabling IPv6 can break parts of Windows and should be done only when there is a very specific reason.

setting.name: IPv6 Stack Disable Override
setting.casual_explanation: IPv6 is the newer network protocol stack that Windows expects to have available. Turning it off can break some network features even if a home network mostly feels like IPv4.
setting.why_people_change_it: People usually touch this only for narrow compatibility, troubleshooting, or legacy network reasons. Microsoft generally recommends leaving IPv6 enabled.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling IPv6 can solve a small number of legacy or misconfigured network scenarios, but it can also create confusing failures in Windows features and modern networking.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents DisabledComponents under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters and explicitly warns against disabling IPv6 unless it is necessary. The current app uses the documented 0xFF value for disabling all IPv6 components except loopback.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: If the value is absent, Windows uses the normal IPv6 stack behavior.

allowed_values.label: Prefer IPv4 over IPv6
allowed_values.meaning: Windows keeps IPv6 available but prefers IPv4 in prefix policies.

allowed_values.label: Disable IPv6 except loopback
allowed_values.meaning: All IPv6 interfaces except loopback are disabled.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisabledComponents = 255, which matches the documented disable-all-except-loopback setting.

windows_defaults.label: Windows IPv6 baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Supported Windows client and server releases covered by Microsoft guidance

states.rationale: Microsoft documents normal IPv6 behavior when DisabledComponents is absent.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft recommends using the default Windows IPv6 configuration unless there is a specific need to override it.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: Environments with a specific documented reason to override IPv6

states.rationale: Leave the override absent and let Windows use its normal IPv6 behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the safest Microsoft-supported baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: A specific network problem has already been traced to IPv6 handling.
tradeoffs: Does not force an IPv4-only posture.

recommended_profiles.label: Disable IPv6
intended_for: Legacy network environments with a documented IPv6 issue

states.rationale: This is the Microsoft-documented value that disables all IPv6 components except loopback.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You have a concrete reason to remove IPv6 from the effective network stack.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You are disabling IPv6 just because it seems unused.
tradeoffs: May help only in specific edge cases.
Can break Windows features and networking assumptions.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Configure IPv6 in Windows
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the DisabledComponents registry value, including 0x20 for preferring IPv4 over IPv6 and 0xFF for disabling all IPv6 components except loopback, and warns that disabling IPv6 is not recommended in general.

evidence.title: Current network tweak provider
evidence.summary: The current app writes DisabledComponents = 255 for this tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft directly documents the DisabledComponents path and the 0xFF value on the same page the record cites. The app writes that exact value, and the restore path is clear even though this remains a high-impact network override.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Location: `HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip6\Parameters\` Name: DisabledComponents Type: REG_DWORD Min Value: 0x00 (default value) Max Value: 0xFF (IPv6 disabled); `Disable IPv6 Decimal 255 Hexadecimal 0xFF`; `We don't recommend that you disable IPv6 or IPv6 components`.
validation_proof.notes: Microsoft directly documents the DisabledComponents registry path and value range, including 0xFF for disabling IPv6 except loopback, and also explicitly warns against disabling IPv6 unless necessary. The app writes DisabledComponents = 255 on that exact documented path.
